question_text: Find the value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{13\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex}
question_html: Find the value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{13\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
We know that, tan-1(tan x) = x ; {tex}x \in \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} and cos-1(cos x) = x, {tex}x \in [0,\;\pi ]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{13\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)} \right] + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\cos \left( {\pi + \frac{{7\pi }}{6}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \cos \frac{{7\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\cos \left( {\pi + \theta } \right) = - \cos \theta } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + \pi - \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\cos \left( {\frac{{7\pi }}{6}} \right)} \right]{/tex}        ....{{tex}\because{/tex} tan-1(-x) = -tan-1 x, x {tex}\in{/tex} R and cos-1(-x) = {tex}\pi{/tex} - cos-1x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1, 1]}
{tex} = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + \pi - {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\cos \left( {\pi + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + \pi - \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( { - \cos \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \cos \left( {\pi - \theta } \right) = - \cos \theta } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + \pi - \pi + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{\pi }{6}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\cos }^{ - 1}}( - x) = \pi - {{\cos }^{ - 1}}x} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = - \frac{\pi }{6} + 0 + \frac{\pi }{6} = 0{/tex}
Note : Remember that, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) \ne \frac{{5\pi }}{6}{/tex} and {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{13\pi }}{6}} \right) \ne \frac{{13\pi }}{6}{/tex}
Since, {tex}\frac{{5\pi }}{6} \notin \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\;\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{13\pi }}{6} \notin [0,\;\pi ]{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\cos\left[ {{{ \cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\cos\left[ {{{ \cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\cos\left[{{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \cos\left[ {{{ \cos }^{ - 1}}\left({\ -cos \frac{{\pi }}{6}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex} {tex} = \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \cos \left( {\frac{{5\pi }}{6} + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)\{ \because {\cos ^{ - 1}}\cos x = x,\;x \in [0,\pi ]\}{/tex} {tex}= \cos \left( {\frac{{6\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex} {tex} = \cos \left( \pi \right) = - 1{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\cos\left[{{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \cos\left[ {{{ \cos }^{ - 1}}\left({\ -cos \frac{{\pi }}{6}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex}

{tex} = \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \cos \left( {\frac{{5\pi }}{6} + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)\{ \because {\cos ^{ - 1}}\cos x = x,\;x \in [0,\pi ]\}{/tex}
{tex}= \cos \left( {\frac{{6\pi }}{6}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = \cos \left( \pi \right) = - 1{/tex}

question_text: Prove that {tex}\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = 7{/tex} .
question_html: Prove that {tex}\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = 7{/tex}.
solution_text: We have to prove, {tex}\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = 7{/tex} {tex} \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = {\cot ^{ - 1}}7{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} - {\cot ^{ - 1}}7{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} = \frac{\pi }{4} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} + ta{n^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right)}^2}}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{\frac{8}{9}}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{3}{4} + \frac{1}{7}}}{{1 - \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{7}}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{{(21 + 4)}}{{28}}}}{{\frac{{(28 - 3)}}{{28}}}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{25}}{{25}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 1 = \tan \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\implies{/tex} 1 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} L.H.S. = R.H.S. Hence Proved .
solution_html:
We have to prove, {tex}\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = 7{/tex}
{tex} \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = {\cot ^{ - 1}}7{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}3} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} - {\cot ^{ - 1}}7{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} = \frac{\pi }{4} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} + ta{n^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right)}^2}}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{\frac{8}{9}}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{3}{4} + \frac{1}{7}}}{{1 - \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{7}}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{{(21 + 4)}}{{28}}}}{{\frac{{(28 - 3)}}{{28}}}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{25}}{{25}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 1 = \tan \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}1 = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} L.H.S. = R.H.S. Hence Proved.

question_text: Find the value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\sin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex} .
question_html: Find the value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\sin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex}.
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\sin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex} . {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{\pi }{3}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}( - 1){/tex} . {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)} \right] + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{3}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}+ ta{n^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{4}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{\pi }{3}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}{/tex} {tex}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{4}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan x} \right) = x,x \in \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right) \hfill \\ {\cot ^{ - 1}}(\cot x) = x,x \in (0,\;\pi ) \hfill \\ and\;{\tan ^{ - 1}}( - x) = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}x \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \frac{\pi }{6} + \frac{\pi }{3} - \frac{\pi }{4} = \frac{{ - 2\pi + 4\pi - 3\pi }}{{12}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 5\pi + 4\pi }}{{12}} = \frac{{ - \pi }}{{12}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\sin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex}.
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{5\pi }}{6}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{\pi }{3}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}( - 1){/tex}.
{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)} \right] + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{3}} \right)} \right]{/tex}{tex}+ ta{n^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{4}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{6}} \right) + {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{\pi }{3}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}{/tex}{tex}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{4}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan x} \right) = x,x \in \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right) \hfill \\ {\cot ^{ - 1}}(\cot x) = x,x \in (0,\;\pi ) \hfill \\ and\;{\tan ^{ - 1}}( - x) = - {\tan ^{ - 1}}x \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= - \frac{\pi }{6} + \frac{\pi }{3} - \frac{\pi }{4} = \frac{{ - 2\pi + 4\pi - 3\pi }}{{12}}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{ - 5\pi + 4\pi }}{{12}} = \frac{{ - \pi }}{{12}}{/tex}

question_html: Find the value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right){/tex}.
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{3}} \right){/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{3}} \right)\;\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}( - x) = - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right]{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\tan \ (\frac{\ - {tex}\pi{/tex} } {3})= - \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan x} \right) = x,\;x \in \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex} Note: Remember that, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right) \ne \frac{{2\pi }}{3}{/tex} Since, tan -1 (tan x) = x, if {tex}x \in \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{2\pi }}{3} \notin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\;\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\tan \left( {\pi - \frac{\pi }{3}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( { - \tan \frac{\pi }{3}} \right)\;\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}( - x) = - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\tan \ (\frac{\ -{tex}\pi{/tex}} {3})= - \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan x} \right) = x,\;x \in \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
Note: Remember that, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right) \ne \frac{{2\pi }}{3}{/tex}
Since, tan-1(tan x) = x, if {tex}x \in \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{2\pi }}{3} \notin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\;\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex}

question_text: Show that 2tan –1 (–3) = {tex}\frac{-\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex}
question_html: Show that 2tan–1 (–3) = {tex}\frac{-\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex}
solution_text: To prove, 2tan -1 (-3) = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} {/tex} + tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex} . LHS, 2tan -1 (-3) = -2tan -1 3 [ Since, tan -1 (-x) = -tan -1 x , x {tex}\in{/tex} R] = {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1} \frac{1-3^{2}}{1+3^{2}}\right]{/tex} [Since, 2tan -1 x = {tex}\left[\cos ^{-1} \frac{1-x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}\right], x \geq 0{/tex} ] = {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-8}{10}\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{5}\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)\right]{/tex} [ Since, cos -1 (-x) = {tex}\pi{/tex} - cos -1 x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1,1]] = {tex}-\pi+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right){/tex} {tex}\left[\operatorname{let} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)=\theta \Rightarrow \cos \theta=\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)\right.{/tex} {tex}\left.\Rightarrow \tan \theta=\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)=-\pi+\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}-\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right){/tex} [ Since, tan -1 (- {tex}x{/tex} ) = -tan -1 {tex}x{/tex} ] ] = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(-\frac{4}{3}\right){/tex} = RHS Hence Proved.
solution_html:
To prove, 2tan-1 (-3) = -{tex}\frac{\pi}{2} {/tex} + tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex}.
LHS, 2tan-1 (-3) = -2tan-1 3 [ Since, tan-1 (-x) = -tan-1x  , x {tex}\in{/tex} R]
= {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1} \frac{1-3^{2}}{1+3^{2}}\right]{/tex} [Since, 2tan-1 x = {tex}\left[\cos ^{-1} \frac{1-x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}\right], x \geq 0{/tex}]
= {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-8}{10}\right)\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{5}\right)\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)\right]{/tex}         [ Since, cos-1(-x) = {tex}\pi{/tex} - cos-1 x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1,1]]
= {tex}-\pi+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right){/tex}             {tex}\left[\operatorname{let} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)=\theta \Rightarrow \cos \theta=\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)\right.{/tex}{tex}\left.\Rightarrow \tan \theta=\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)=-\pi+\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}-\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right){/tex}
[ Since, tan-1 (-{tex}x{/tex}) = -tan-1 {tex}x{/tex}] ]
= {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{-4}{3}\right){/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}+\tan ^{-1}\left(-\frac{4}{3}\right){/tex} = RHS
Hence Proved.

question_text: Find the real Solution of the equation {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {/tex} {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
question_html: Find the real Solution of the equation {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {/tex}{tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {/tex} {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} ...(i) Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \theta {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan \theta = \frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \tan \theta = \frac{{\sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta }}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\because \theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }}{/tex} {tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1}{/tex} Putting the value of {tex}\theta{/tex} in Equation (i), {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} We know that, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}y {/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right),\;xy < 1{/tex} {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - {x^2} - x}}} }}{{1 - \sqrt {x(x + 1)} \cdot \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - {x^2} - x}}} }}} \right] = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x} + \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - 1({x^2} + x)}}} }}{{1 - \sqrt {{x^2} + x} \cdot \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - 1({x^2} + x)}}} }}} \right] = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{x^2} + x + \sqrt { - ({x^2} + x + 1)} }}{{\left[ {\sqrt{(x^2+x)} - \sqrt { - ({x^2} + x + 1)} \sqrt {({x^2} + x)} } \right]}} = \tan \frac{\pi }{2} = \frac{1}{0}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {1 - \sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} + x + 1} \right)} } \right]\sqrt {\left( {{x^2} + x} \right)} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} -(x^2 + x + 1) = 1 \quad or\quad x^2 + x = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-x^2 - x - 1 = 1 \quad or\quad x(x + 1) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^2 + x + 2 = 0 \quad or\quad x(x + 1) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0 or x = -1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} For real solution, we have x= 0, -1.
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {/tex} {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} ...(i)
Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} = \theta {/tex}


{tex} \Rightarrow \tan \theta = \frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }}{/tex}  {tex}\left[ {\because \tan \theta = \frac{{\sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta }}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\because \theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }}{/tex}
{tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1}{/tex}
Putting the value of {tex}\theta{/tex} in Equation (i), 
{tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x + 1} }}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - x} }} = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
We know that, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}y {/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right),\;xy < 1{/tex}
{tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {x(x + 1)} + \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - {x^2} - x}}} }}{{1 - \sqrt {x(x + 1)} \cdot \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - {x^2} - x}}} }}} \right] = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
{tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + x} + \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - 1({x^2} + x)}}} }}{{1 - \sqrt {{x^2} + x} \cdot \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + x + 1}}{{ - 1({x^2} + x)}}} }}} \right] = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{x^2} + x + \sqrt { - ({x^2} + x + 1)} }}{{\left[ {\sqrt{(x^2+x)} - \sqrt { - ({x^2} + x + 1)} \sqrt {({x^2} + x)} } \right]}} = \tan \frac{\pi }{2} = \frac{1}{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {1 - \sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} + x + 1} \right)} } \right]\sqrt {\left( {{x^2} + x} \right)} = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex} -(x^2 + x + 1) = 1 \quad or\quad x^2 + x = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-x^2 - x - 1 = 1 \quad or\quad x(x + 1) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^2 + x + 2 = 0 \quad or\quad x(x + 1) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0 or x = -1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} For real solution, we have x= 0, -1.

question_text: Find the value of {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}2\sqrt 2 } \right){/tex}
question_html: Find the value of {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}2\sqrt 2 } \right){/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}2\sqrt 2 } \right){/tex} {tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left\{ {\frac{{2 \times \frac{1}{3}}}{{1 + {{\left\{ {\frac{1}{3}} \right\}}^2}}}} \right\}} \right] + \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}},\; - 1 \leqslant x \leqslant 1} \right.{/tex} {tex}\left. {and\;{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {2\sqrt 2 } \right) = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 + \frac{1}{9}}}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\left\{ {\because \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = x,\;x \in [ - 1,1]} \right\}{/tex} {tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2 \times 9}}{{3 \times 10}}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x = x} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{3}{5} + \frac{1}{3} = \frac{{9 + 5}}{{15}} = \frac{{14}}{{15}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}2\sqrt 2 } \right){/tex}
{tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left\{ {\frac{{2 \times \frac{1}{3}}}{{1 + {{\left\{ {\frac{1}{3}} \right\}}^2}}}} \right\}} \right] + \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}} \right]{/tex}  {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}},\; - 1 \leqslant x \leqslant 1} \right.{/tex} {tex}\left. {and\;{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {2\sqrt 2 } \right) = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 + \frac{1}{9}}}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex}   {tex}\left\{ {\because \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = x,\;x \in [ - 1,1]} \right\}{/tex}
{tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2 \times 9}}{{3 \times 10}}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex} = \sin \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}} \right)} \right] + \frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x = x} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{3}{5} + \frac{1}{3} = \frac{{9 + 5}}{{15}} = \frac{{14}}{{15}}{/tex}

question_text: If {tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}(\cos \theta ) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}(2\ cosec\theta ){/tex} then show that {tex}\theta = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} , where n is any integer.
question_html: If {tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}(\cos \theta ) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}(2\ cosec\theta ){/tex} then show that {tex}\theta = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}, where n is any integer.
solution_text: We have, {tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}(\cos \theta ) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}(2\cos ec\theta ){/tex} , {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\cos \theta }}{{1 - {{\cos }^2}\theta }}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{2\cos \theta }}{{{{\sin }^2}\theta }}} \right) = \left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\cot \theta .2\cos ec\theta } \right) = \left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cot \theta = 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cot \theta = \cot \frac{\pi }{4} \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}(\cos \theta ) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}(2\cos ec\theta ){/tex},
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\cos \theta }}{{1 - {{\cos }^2}\theta }}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{2\cos \theta }}{{{{\sin }^2}\theta }}} \right) = \left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\cot \theta .2\cos ec\theta } \right) = \left( {2\cos ec\theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cot \theta = 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cot \theta = \cot \frac{\pi }{4} \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

question_text: Show that {tex}\cos \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}} \right) = \sin \left( {4{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex}
question_html: Show that {tex}\cos \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}} \right) = \sin \left( {4{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\cos \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}} \right) = \sin \left( {4{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{7}} \right)}^2}}}{{1 + {{\left( {\frac{1}{7}} \right)}^2}}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {2.2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\ 2tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{{48}}{{49}}}}{{\frac{{50}}{{49}}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \sin \left[ {2 \cdot \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right)}^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{48 \times 49}}{{50 \times 49}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left[ {2{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{18}}{{24}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{24}}{{25}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{24}}{{25}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2 \times \frac{3}{4}}}{{1 + \frac{9}{{16}}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{24}}{{25}} = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{3}{2}}}{{\frac{{25}}{{16}}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\frac{{24}}{{25}} = \frac{{48}}{{50}} \Rightarrow \frac{{24}}{{25}} = \frac{{24}}{{25}}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} L.H.S. = R.H.S. (hence proved)
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\cos \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7}} \right) = \sin \left( {4{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{7}} \right)}^2}}}{{1 + {{\left( {\frac{1}{7}} \right)}^2}}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {2.2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\ 2tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{{48}}{{49}}}}{{\frac{{50}}{{49}}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \sin \left[ {2 \cdot \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right)}^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{48 \times 49}}{{50 \times 49}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left[ {2{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{18}}{{24}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{24}}{{25}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left[ {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{24}}{{25}}} \right)} \right] = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2 \times \frac{3}{4}}}{{1 + \frac{9}{{16}}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{24}}{{25}} = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{3}{2}}}{{\frac{{25}}{{16}}}}} \right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{{24}}{{25}} = \frac{{48}}{{50}} \Rightarrow \frac{{24}}{{25}} = \frac{{24}}{{25}}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} L.H.S. = R.H.S. (hence proved)

question_text: Solve the following equation {tex}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex} .
question_html: Solve the following equation {tex}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex}.
solution_text: We have, {tex}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex} . {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5}} \right){/tex} Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = {\theta _1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan {\theta _1} = \frac{x}{1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos {\theta _1} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }} \Rightarrow {\theta _1} = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}{/tex} And {tex}{\cot ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = {\theta _2} \Rightarrow \cot {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin {\theta _2} = \frac{4}{5} \Rightarrow {\theta _2} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }} = \frac{4}{5}{/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} cos(cos -1 x) = x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1, 1] and sin(sin -1 x) = x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1, 1]) On Squaring both sides, we get {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16(x 2 + 1) = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16x 2 = 9 {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} = {\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore x = \pm \frac{3}{4} = \frac{{ - 3}}{4},\;\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}} \right) = \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5}} \right){/tex}
Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = {\theta _1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan {\theta _1} = \frac{x}{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos {\theta _1} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }} \Rightarrow {\theta _1} = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}{/tex}
And {tex}{\cot ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = {\theta _2} \Rightarrow \cot {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin {\theta _2} = \frac{4}{5} \Rightarrow {\theta _2} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }} = \frac{4}{5}{/tex}
({tex}\because{/tex} cos(cos-1x) = x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1, 1] and sin(sin-1x) = x, x {tex}\in{/tex} [-1, 1])
On Squaring both sides, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16(x2 + 1) = 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16x2 = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} = {\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore x = \pm \frac{3}{4} = \frac{{ - 3}}{4},\;\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

question_text: Prove that {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}
question_html: Prove that {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex} L.H.S. {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex} ......(i) {tex}\left[ {let\;{x^2} = \cos 2\theta = \left( {{{\cos }^2}\theta - {{\sin }^2}\theta } \right)} \right.{/tex} {tex}\left. { = 1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta = 2{{\cos }^2}\theta - 1} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2} = 2\theta \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} = \sqrt {1 + \cos 2\theta }{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt {1 + 2co{s^2}\theta - 1} = \sqrt 2 \cos \theta{/tex} And {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} = \sqrt {1 - \cos 2\theta }{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt {1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } = \sqrt 2 \sin \theta{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 \cos \theta + \sqrt 2 \sin \theta }}{{\sqrt 2 \cos \theta - \sqrt 2 \sin \theta }}} \right){/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\cos \theta + \sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta - \sin \theta }}} \right){/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 + \tan \theta }}{{1 - \tan \theta }}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) + \tan \theta }}{{1 - \tan \frac{\pi }{4} \cdot \tan \theta }}} \right){/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} + \theta } \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\tan x + \tan y}}{{1 - \tan x.\tan y}}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{\pi }{4} + \theta = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex} = R.H.S. Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have,
{tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}
L.H.S. {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex}......(i)
{tex}\left[ {let\;{x^2} = \cos 2\theta = \left( {{{\cos }^2}\theta - {{\sin }^2}\theta } \right)} \right.{/tex}{tex}\left. { = 1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta = 2{{\cos }^2}\theta - 1} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2} = 2\theta \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} = \sqrt {1 + \cos 2\theta }{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt {1 + 2co{s^2}\theta - 1} = \sqrt 2 \cos \theta{/tex}
And {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} = \sqrt {1 - \cos 2\theta }{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt {1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } = \sqrt 2 \sin \theta{/tex}

 {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 \cos \theta + \sqrt 2 \sin \theta }}{{\sqrt 2 \cos \theta - \sqrt 2 \sin \theta }}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\cos \theta + \sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta - \sin \theta }}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 + \tan \theta }}{{1 - \tan \theta }}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) + \tan \theta }}{{1 - \tan \frac{\pi }{4} \cdot \tan \theta }}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} + \theta } \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\tan x + \tan y}}{{1 - \tan x.\tan y}}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{\pi }{4} + \theta = \frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{1}{2}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}
= R.H.S. Hence proved.

question_text: Find the simplified form of {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}\cos x + \frac{4}{5}\sin x} \right){/tex} , where {tex}x \in \left[ {\frac{{ - 3\pi }}{4},\;\frac{\pi }{4}} \right]{/tex}
question_html: Find the simplified form of {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}\cos x + \frac{4}{5}\sin x} \right){/tex}, where {tex}x \in \left[ {\frac{{ - 3\pi }}{4},\;\frac{\pi }{4}} \right]{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}\cos x + \frac{4}{5}\sin x} \right),x \in \left[ {\frac{{ - 3\pi }}{4},\;\frac{\pi }{4}} \right]{/tex} Let {tex}\cos y = \frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{4}{5}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{4}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} cos -1 [cosy.cosx + siny.sinx] = cos-1[cos(y - x)] [as cos(A - B) = cosA.cosB + sinA.sinB] {tex} = y - x = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3} - x\left[ {\because y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{5}\cos x + \frac{4}{5}\sin x} \right),x \in \left[ {\frac{{ - 3\pi }}{4},\;\frac{\pi }{4}} \right]{/tex}
Let {tex}\cos y = \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{4}{5}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow y = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{5} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{4}{3}} \right){/tex}

question_html: Prove that {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}.
solution_text: We have to prove that, {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} . {tex}\therefore{/tex} L.H.S. {tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{15}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} = {\theta _1} \Rightarrow \sin {\theta _1} = \frac{8}{{17}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan {\theta _1} = \frac{8}{{15}} \Rightarrow {\theta _1} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{15}}{/tex} And {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\theta _2} \Rightarrow \sin {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow {\theta _2} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{8}{{15}} + \frac{3}{4}}}{{1 - \frac{8}{{15}} \times \frac{3}{4}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{32 + 45}}{{60}}}}{{\frac{{60 - 24}}{{60}}}}} \right] = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{77}}{{36}}} \right){/tex} Let {tex}{\theta _3} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{36}} \Rightarrow \tan {\theta _3} = \frac{{77}}{{36}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin {\theta _3} = \frac{{77}}{\sqrt {5929+ 1296}} = \frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {\theta _{_3}} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} {tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} = R.H.S. Hence proved. Alternate Method To Prove, {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} = x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{8}{{17}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos x = \sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}x} = \sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{8}{{17}}} \right)}^2}}{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt {\frac{{289 - 64}}{{289}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{225}}{{289}}} = \frac{{15}}{{17}}{/tex} Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = y{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{3}{5} \Rightarrow si{n^2}y = \frac{9}{{25}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {\cos ^2}y = 1 - \frac{9}{{25}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^2}y = {\left( {\frac{4}{5}} \right)^2} \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{4}{5}{/tex} Now, sin(x + y) = sinx.cosy + cosx.siny {tex} = \frac{8}{{17}} \cdot \frac{4}{5} + \frac{{15}}{{17}} \cdot \frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{31}}{{85}} + \frac{{45}}{{85}} = \frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (x + y) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{77}}{{85}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have to prove that, {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.H.S. {tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{15}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} = {\theta _1} \Rightarrow \sin {\theta _1} = \frac{8}{{17}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \tan {\theta _1} = \frac{8}{{15}} \Rightarrow {\theta _1} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{15}}{/tex}
And {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\theta _2} \Rightarrow \sin {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan {\theta _2} = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow {\theta _2} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{8}{{15}} + \frac{3}{4}}}{{1 - \frac{8}{{15}} \times \frac{3}{4}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{32 + 45}}{{60}}}}{{\frac{{60 - 24}}{{60}}}}} \right] = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{77}}{{36}}} \right){/tex}

Let {tex}{\theta _3} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{36}} \Rightarrow \tan {\theta _3} = \frac{{77}}{{36}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin {\theta _3} = \frac{{77}}{\sqrt {5929+ 1296}} = \frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {\theta _{_3}} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex} = R.H.S. Hence proved.
Alternate Method
To Prove, {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}

Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} = x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{8}{{17}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \cos x = \sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}x} = \sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{8}{{17}}} \right)}^2}}{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt {\frac{{289 - 64}}{{289}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{225}}{{289}}} = \frac{{15}}{{17}}{/tex}
Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = y{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{3}{5} \Rightarrow si{n^2}y = \frac{9}{{25}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {\cos ^2}y = 1 - \frac{9}{{25}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^2}y = {\left( {\frac{4}{5}} \right)^2} \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{4}{5}{/tex}
Now, sin(x + y) = sinx.cosy + cosx.siny
{tex} = \frac{8}{{17}} \cdot \frac{4}{5} + \frac{{15}}{{17}} \cdot \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{31}}{{85}} + \frac{{45}}{{85}} = \frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (x + y) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{77}}{{85}}} \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}} + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{77}}{{85}}{/tex}

question_html: Show that {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{13}} + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex}.
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{13}} + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex} .....(i) Let {tex}si{n^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{13}} = x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{5}{{13}}{/tex} And cos 2 x = 1 - sin 2 x {tex}= 1 - \frac{{25}}{{169}} = \frac{{144}}{{169}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos x = \sqrt {\frac{{144}}{{169}}} = \frac{{12}}{{13}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \tan x = \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}} = \frac{{\frac{5}{{13}}}}{{\frac{{12}}{{13}}}} = \frac{5}{{12}}{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \tan x = \frac{5}{{12}}{/tex} ...(iii) Again, Let {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = y \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \sin y = \sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}y}{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{3}{5}} \right)}^2}} = \sqrt {1 - \frac{9}{{25}}}{/tex} {tex}\sin y = \sqrt {\frac{{16}}{{25}}} = \frac{4}{5}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan y = \frac{{\sin y}}{{\cos y}} = \frac{{\frac{4}{5}}}{{\frac{3}{5}}} = \frac{4}{3}{/tex} ...(iv) We know that, {tex}\tan (x + y) = \frac{{\tan x + \tan y}}{{1 - \tan x.\tan y}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{5}{{12}} + \frac{4}{3}}}{{1 - \frac{5}{{12}} \cdot \frac{4}{3}}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{{15 + 48}}{{36}}}}{{\frac{{36 - 20}}{{36}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{{63}}{{36}}}}{{\frac{{16}}{{36}}}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x + y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{12}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have,
{tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{13}} + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex} .....(i)

Let
{tex}si{n^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{13}} = x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{5}{{13}}{/tex}
And cos2x = 1 - sin2x
{tex}= 1 - \frac{{25}}{{169}} = \frac{{144}}{{169}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos x = \sqrt {\frac{{144}}{{169}}} = \frac{{12}}{{13}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \tan x = \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}} = \frac{{\frac{5}{{13}}}}{{\frac{{12}}{{13}}}} = \frac{5}{{12}}{/tex} ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan x = \frac{5}{{12}}{/tex} ...(iii)
Again, Let
{tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = y \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \sin y = \sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}y}{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{3}{5}} \right)}^2}} = \sqrt {1 - \frac{9}{{25}}}{/tex}
{tex}\sin y = \sqrt {\frac{{16}}{{25}}} = \frac{4}{5}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \tan y = \frac{{\sin y}}{{\cos y}} = \frac{{\frac{4}{5}}}{{\frac{3}{5}}} = \frac{4}{3}{/tex} ...(iv)

We know that,
{tex}\tan (x + y) = \frac{{\tan x + \tan y}}{{1 - \tan x.\tan y}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{5}{{12}} + \frac{4}{3}}}{{1 - \frac{5}{{12}} \cdot \frac{4}{3}}}{/tex}{tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{{15 + 48}}{{36}}}}{{\frac{{36 - 20}}{{36}}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{\frac{{63}}{{36}}}}{{\frac{{16}}{{36}}}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \tan (x + y) = \frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x + y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{12}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{63}}{{16}}{/tex}
Hence proved.

question_text: Prove that {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex} .
question_html: Prove that {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}.
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex} Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} = x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan x = \frac{1}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^2}x = \frac{1}{{16}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}x - 1 = \frac{1}{{16}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sec ^2}x = 1 + \frac{1}{{16}} = \frac{{17}}{{16}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}x}} = \frac{{17}}{{16}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\cos ^2}x = \frac{{16}}{{17}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos x = \frac{4}{{\sqrt {17} }}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^2}x = 1 - {\cos ^2}x = 1 - \frac{{16}}{{17}} = \frac{1}{{17}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {17} }}{/tex} ....(ii) Again, let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan y = \frac{2}{9} \Rightarrow {\tan ^2}y = \frac{4}{{81}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}y - 1 = \frac{4}{{81}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}y = \frac{4}{{81}} + 1 = \frac{{85}}{{81}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^2}y = \frac{{81}}{{85}} \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{9}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^2}y = 1 - {\cos ^2}y = 1 - \frac{{81}}{{85}} = \frac{4}{{85}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{2}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex} ...(iii) We know that, sin(x + y) = sinx.cosy + cosx.siny {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {17} }} \cdot \frac{9}{{\sqrt {85} }} + \frac{4}{{\sqrt {17} }}.\frac{2}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{17}}{{\sqrt {17} \cdot \sqrt {85} }} = \frac{{\sqrt {17} }}{{\sqrt {17} \cdot \sqrt 5 }} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (x + y) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}
Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} = x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \tan x = \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^2}x = \frac{1}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}x - 1 = \frac{1}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\sec ^2}x = 1 + \frac{1}{{16}} = \frac{{17}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}x}} = \frac{{17}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\cos ^2}x = \frac{{16}}{{17}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos x = \frac{4}{{\sqrt {17} }}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^2}x = 1 - {\cos ^2}x = 1 - \frac{{16}}{{17}} = \frac{1}{{17}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {17} }}{/tex} ....(ii)
Again, let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan y = \frac{2}{9} \Rightarrow {\tan ^2}y = \frac{4}{{81}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}y - 1 = \frac{4}{{81}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\sec ^2}y = \frac{4}{{81}} + 1 = \frac{{85}}{{81}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^2}y = \frac{{81}}{{85}} \Rightarrow \cos y = \frac{9}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^2}y = 1 - {\cos ^2}y = 1 - \frac{{81}}{{85}} = \frac{4}{{85}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin y = \frac{2}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex} ...(iii)
We know that, sin(x + y) = sinx.cosy + cosx.siny
{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {17} }} \cdot \frac{9}{{\sqrt {85} }} + \frac{4}{{\sqrt {17} }}.\frac{2}{{\sqrt {85} }}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{17}}{{\sqrt {17} \cdot \sqrt {85} }} = \frac{{\sqrt {17} }}{{\sqrt {17} \cdot \sqrt 5 }} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (x + y) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{9} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}

Hence proved.

question_html: Find the value of {tex}4{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}4{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex}= 2.2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex}= 2\left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{\frac{2}{5}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{1}{5}} \right)}^2}}}} \right] - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = 2.\left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{2}{5}}}{{1 - \frac{1}{{25}}}}} \right)} \right] - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex} = 2.\left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{2}{5}}}{{\frac{{24}}{{25}}}}} \right)} \right] - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex}= 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{5}{{12}} - ta{n^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{2.\frac{5}{{12}}}}{{1 - {{\left( {\frac{5}{{12}}} \right)}^2}}} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{1 - \frac{{25}}{{144}}}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{144 \times 5}}{{119 \times 6}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{120}}{{199}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{239}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{{120}}{{119}} - \frac{1}{{239}}}}{{1 + \frac{{120}}{{119}} \cdot \frac{1}{{239}}}}} \right){/tex}{tex}\left[ {\because \;{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x - y}}{{1 + xy}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{120 \times 239 - 119}}{{119 \times 239 \times 120}}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{28680 - 119}}{{28441 + 120}}} \right] = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{28561}}{{28561}}{/tex}
{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}(1) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{4}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

question_html: Show that {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) = \frac{{4 - \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex} and justify why the other  value {tex}\frac{{4 + \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex} is ignored?
solution_text: We have, {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) = \frac{{4 - \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} LHS {tex}= \tan \left[ {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)} \right]{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{1}{2}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = \theta \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = 2\theta{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin 2\theta = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow \frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }} = \frac{3}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 + 3{\tan ^2}\theta = 8\tan \theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3{\tan ^2}\theta - 8\tan \theta + 3 = 0{/tex} Let {tex}\tan \theta = y{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} 3y 2 - 8y + 3 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow y = \frac{{ + 8 \pm \sqrt {64 - 4 \times 3 \times 3} }}{{2 \times 3}} = \frac{{8 \pm \sqrt {28} }}{6}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{2\left[ {4 \pm \sqrt 7 } \right]}}{{2.3}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan \theta = \frac{{4 \pm \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{4 \pm \sqrt 7 }}{3}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left\{ {{\text{but }}\frac{{4 + \sqrt 7 }}{3} > {1},{\text{since max}}} \right.{/tex} {tex}\left. {\left[ {\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right)} \right] = 1} \right\}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} L.H.S. = {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) = \frac{{4 - \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex} {tex}{/tex} = RHS Note Since , {tex} - \frac{\pi }{2} \leqslant {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} \leqslant \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow - \frac{\pi }{4} \leqslant \frac{1}{2}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} \leqslant \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \tan \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{4}} \right) \leqslant \tan \frac{1}{2}\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) \leqslant \tan \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow - 1 \leqslant \tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) \leqslant 1{/tex}
solution_html:
We have,
{tex}\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) = \frac{{4 - \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} LHS {tex}= \tan \left[ {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
Let {tex}\frac{1}{2}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = \theta \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} = 2\theta{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin 2\theta = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow \frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }} = \frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 3 + 3{\tan ^2}\theta = 8\tan \theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 3{\tan ^2}\theta - 8\tan \theta + 3 = 0{/tex}
Let {tex}\tan \theta = y{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3y2 - 8y + 3 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow y = \frac{{ + 8 \pm \sqrt {64 - 4 \times 3 \times 3} }}{{2 \times 3}} = \frac{{8 \pm \sqrt {28} }}{6}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{2\left[ {4 \pm \sqrt 7 } \right]}}{{2.3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan \theta = \frac{{4 \pm \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{4 \pm \sqrt 7 }}{3}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\left\{ {{\text{but }}\frac{{4 + \sqrt 7 }}{3} > {1},{\text{since max}}} \right.{/tex}{tex}\left. {\left[ {\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right)} \right] = 1} \right\}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.H.S. =    {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) = \frac{{4 - \sqrt 7 }}{3}{/tex}{tex}{/tex} = RHS
Note Since, {tex} - \frac{\pi }{2} \leqslant {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} \leqslant \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow - \frac{\pi }{4} \leqslant \frac{1}{2}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} \leqslant \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \tan \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{4}} \right) \leqslant \tan \frac{1}{2}\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) \leqslant \tan \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow - 1 \leqslant \tan \left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right) \leqslant 1{/tex}

question_html:
If a1, a2, a3, ............ an is an arithmetic progression with common difference d, then evaluate the following expression:
{tex}\tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}} \right.\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_1}{a_2}}}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_2}{a_3}}}} \right)+{/tex}{tex} {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left. {\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_3}{a_4}}}} \right) + .... + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_{n - 1}}{a_n}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
solution_text: We have, a 1 = a, a 2 = a + d, a 3 = a + 2d ... And d = a 2 - a 1 = a 3 - a2 = a 4 - a 3 = .... = a n - a n-1 Given that, {tex}\tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}} \right.\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_1}{a_2}}}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_2}{a_3}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}+ {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left. {\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_3}{a_4}}}} \right) + .... + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_{n - 1}}{a_n}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_2} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_2}.{a_1}}} + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_3} - {a_2}}}{{1 + {a_3}.{a_2}}}} \right.{/tex} {tex}\left. { + ... + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_n} - {a_{n - 1}}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_{n - 1}}}}} \right]{/tex} = tan [(tan -1 a 2 - tan -1 a 1) + (tan -1 a 3 - tan -1 a 2 ) + .... + (tan -1 a n - tan -1 a n-1 )] = tan [tan -1 a n - tan -1 a 1 ] {tex} = \tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_n} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_1}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}} - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x - y}}{{1 + xy}}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{a_n} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_1}}}\;\left[ {\because \tan \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = x} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, a1 = a, a2 = a + d, a3 = a + 2d ...
And d = a2 - a1 = a3 - a2 = a4 - a3 = .... = an - an-1
Given that, {tex}\tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}} \right.\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_1}{a_2}}}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_2}{a_3}}}} \right){/tex}{tex}+ {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left. {\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_3}{a_4}}}} \right) + .... + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{d}{{1 + {a_{n - 1}}{a_n}}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_2} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_2}.{a_1}}} + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_3} - {a_2}}}{{1 + {a_3}.{a_2}}}} \right.{/tex}{tex}\left. { + ... + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_n} - {a_{n - 1}}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_{n - 1}}}}} \right]{/tex}
= tan [(tan-1a2 - tan-1a1) + (tan-1a3 - tan-1a2) + .... + (tan-1an - tan-1an-1)]
= tan [tan-1an - tan-1a1]
{tex} = \tan \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{a_n} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_1}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {{\tan }^{ - 1}} - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x - y}}{{1 + xy}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{{a_n} - {a_1}}}{{1 + {a_n}.{a_1}}}\;\left[ {\because \tan \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = x} \right]{/tex}

question_html:
The value of the expression tan {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}\right){/tex} is

options.explanation: We need to find, {tex}\tan \frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}{/tex} Let, {tex}\cos ^{-1} \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}=A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos A=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}{/tex} Also, we need to find tan {tex}\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}{/tex} We know that, {tex}\tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\sqrt{\frac{(1-\cos \theta)}{1+\cos \theta}}{/tex} So, {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{A}{2}=\sqrt{\frac{(1-\cos A)}{1+\cos A}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{1-\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}}{1+\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{\frac{\sqrt{5}-2}{\sqrt{5}}}{\frac{\sqrt{5}+2}{\sqrt{5}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{\sqrt{5}-2}{\sqrt{5}+2}}{/tex} On rationalizing, {tex}\tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{(\sqrt{5}-2)(\sqrt{5}+2)}{(\sqrt{5}+2)(\sqrt{5}+2)}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{(\sqrt{5})^{2}-2^{2}}{(\sqrt{5}+2)^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{5-4}{(\sqrt{5}+2)^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{\frac{1}{(\sqrt{5}+2)^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}+2}{/tex} Again rationalizing, {tex}\tan ^{-1} A=\frac{1(\sqrt{5}-2)}{(\sqrt{5}+2)(\sqrt{5}-2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\frac{(\sqrt{5}-2)}{\left(\sqrt{5}^{2}-2^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\frac{(\sqrt{5}-2)}{(5-4)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} A=\sqrt{5}-2{/tex}

question_text: Find the principal value of cos -1 x, for {tex}x = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}{/tex} .
question_html: Find the principal value of cos-1x, for {tex}x = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}{/tex}.
solution_text: If {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) = \theta {/tex} , then {tex}\cos \theta = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}{/tex} . Since, we are considering principal branch, {tex}\theta \in [0,\pi ]{/tex} . Also, since {tex}\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} > 0{/tex} , {tex}\theta{/tex} being in the first quadrant, hence {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} .
solution_html:
If {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) = \theta {/tex}, then {tex}\cos \theta = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}{/tex}.
Since, we are considering principal branch, {tex}\theta \in [0,\pi ]{/tex}. Also, since {tex}\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} > 0{/tex}, {tex}\theta{/tex} being in the first quadrant, hence {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex}.

question_text: Prove that tan(cot -1 x) = cot(tan -1 x). State with reason whether the equality is valid for all values of x.
question_html: Prove that tan(cot-1x) = cot(tan-1x). State with reason whether the equality is valid for all values of x.
solution_text: Let cot -1 x = {tex}\theta{/tex} . Then cot {tex}\theta{/tex} = x Or, {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - \theta } \right) = x \Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{2} - \theta {/tex} So {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \tan \theta = \cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - \theta } \right){/tex} {tex} = \cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - {{\cot }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \cot \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right){/tex} The equality is valid for all values of x since tan -1 x and cot -1 x are true for x {tex}\in{/tex} R.
solution_html:
Let cot-1x = {tex}\theta{/tex}. Then cot {tex}\theta{/tex} = x
Or, {tex}\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - \theta } \right) = x \Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{2} - \theta {/tex}
So {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \tan \theta = \cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - \theta } \right){/tex}
{tex} = \cot \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - {{\cot }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \cot \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right){/tex}
The equality is valid for all values of x since tan-1x and cot-1x are true for x {tex}\in{/tex} R.

question_text: Find value of tan (cos -1 x) and hence evaluate {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}}} \right){/tex}
question_html: Find value of tan (cos-1x) and hence evaluate {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}}} \right){/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \theta{/tex} , then {tex}\cos \theta = x{/tex} , where {tex}\theta \in [0,\pi ]{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \tan \theta{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}\theta } }}{{\cos \theta }} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{x}{/tex} Hence, {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}}} \right) = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{8}{{17}}} \right)}^2}} }}{{\frac{8}{{17}}}} = \frac{{15}}{8}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \theta{/tex}, then {tex}\cos \theta = x{/tex}, where {tex}\theta \in [0,\pi ]{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \tan \theta{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}\theta } }}{{\cos \theta }} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{x}{/tex}
Hence, {tex}\tan \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{8}{{17}}} \right) = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {{\left( {\frac{8}{{17}}} \right)}^2}} }}{{\frac{8}{{17}}}} = \frac{{15}}{8}{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}{\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - 5}}{{12}}} \right) = y{/tex} Then {tex}\cot y = \frac{{ - 5}}{{12}}{/tex} Now, {tex}\sin \left[ {2{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - 5}}{{12}}} \right)} \right] = \sin 2y{/tex} {tex}= 2\sin y\cos y = 2\left( {\frac{{12}}{{13}}} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - 5}}{{13}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {{\text{since}}\;\cot y < 0,\;{\text{so}}\;y \in \left( {\frac{\pi }{2},\pi } \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 120}}{{169}}{/tex}

Then {tex}\cot y = \frac{{ - 5}}{{12}}{/tex}
Now,

{tex}= 2\sin y\cos y = 2\left( {\frac{{12}}{{13}}} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - 5}}{{13}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {{\text{since}}\;\cot y < 0,\;{\text{so}}\;y \in \left( {\frac{\pi }{2},\pi } \right)} \right]{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\cos \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {{\sec }^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3}} \right]{/tex} = {tex} \cos \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}} \right)\cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex} {tex} - \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}} \right)\sin \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{3}{4}\sqrt {1 - {{ (\frac{1}{4}})^2}} - \frac{1}{4}\sqrt {1 - {{(\frac{3}{4}})^2}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{3}{4}\frac{{\sqrt {15} }}{4} - \frac{1}{4}\frac{{\sqrt 7 }}{4} = \frac{{3\sqrt {15} - \sqrt 7 }}{{16}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\cos \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {{\sec }^{ - 1}}\frac{4}{3}} \right]{/tex} = {tex} \cos \left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4} + {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}} \right)\cos \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex}{tex} - \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{4}} \right)\sin \left( {{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{3}{4}\sqrt {1 - {{ (\frac{1}{4}})^2}} - \frac{1}{4}\sqrt {1 - {{(\frac{3}{4}})^2}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Prove that {tex}2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} .
question_html: Prove that {tex}2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}.
solution_text: Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = \theta{/tex} , then {tex}\sin \theta = \frac{3}{5}{/tex} , where {tex}\theta \in \left[ {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right]{/tex} Thus, {tex}\tan \theta = \frac{3}{4}{/tex} , which gives {tex}\theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex} {tex} = 2\theta - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}} = 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex} {tex}={\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2.\frac{3}{4}}}{{1 - \frac{9}{{16}}}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{24}}{7} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{{24}}{7} - \frac{{17}}{{31}}}}{{1 + \frac{{24}}{7} \cdot \frac{{17}}{{31}}}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{625}{625}\right)\\ =\tan ^{-1} 1 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} = \theta{/tex}, then
{tex}\sin \theta = \frac{3}{5}{/tex},
where {tex}\theta \in \left[ {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right]{/tex}
Thus,
{tex}\tan \theta = \frac{3}{4}{/tex}, which gives {tex}\theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
Therefore,
{tex}2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{5} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex}
{tex} = 2\theta - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}} = 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{4} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex}
{tex}={\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2.\frac{3}{4}}}{{1 - \frac{9}{{16}}}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex}{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{24}}{7} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{17}}{{31}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{{24}}{7} - \frac{{17}}{{31}}}}{{1 + \frac{{24}}{7} \cdot \frac{{17}}{{31}}}}} \right) {/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{625}{625}\right)\\ =\tan ^{-1} 1 {/tex}
{tex}= \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

question_text: Prove that cot -1 7 + cot -1 8 + cot -1 18 = cot -1 3
question_html: Prove that cot-17 + cot-18 + cot-118 = cot-13
solution_html:
We have cot-17 + cot-18 + cot-118
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{7} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{8} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{18}}{/tex} {tex}\left( {{\text{since}}\;{{\cot }^{ - 1}}x = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{x},\;{\text{if}}\;x > 0} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{1}{7} + \frac{1}{8}}}{{1 - \frac{1}{7} \times \frac{1}{8}}}} \right) + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{18}}{/tex} {tex}\left( {{\text{since }}x,y = \frac{1}{7} \cdot \frac{1}{8} < 1} \right){/tex}
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{3}{{11}} + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{{18}} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{3}{{11}} + \frac{1}{{18}}}}{{1 - \frac{3}{{11}} \times \frac{1}{{18}}}}} \right){/tex} (since xy < 1)
{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{65}}{{195}} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}3{/tex}

question_text: Which is greater, tan 1 or tan -1 1?
question_html: Which is greater, tan 1 or tan-1 1?
solution_text: From Fig. we note that tan x is an increasing function in the interval {tex}\left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\;\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} , since {tex}1 > \frac{\pi }{4} \Rightarrow \tan 1 > \tan \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} . This gives tan 1 > 1 {tex} \Rightarrow \tan 1 > 1 > \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan 1 > 1 > {\tan ^{ - 1}}(1){/tex}
solution_html:

tan 1 > 1
{tex} \Rightarrow \tan 1 > 1 > \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan 1 > 1 > {\tan ^{ - 1}}(1){/tex}

question_html: Find the value of {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt 3 } \right){/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{3} = x{/tex} and {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt 3 = y{/tex} so that {tex}\tan x = \frac{2}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\tan y = \sqrt 3{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt 3 } \right){/tex} = sin(2x) + cos y {tex} = \frac{{2\tan x}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}x}} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 + {{\tan }^2}y} }}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{2.\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 + \frac{4}{9}}} + \frac{1} { \sqrt{1 + {{{\left( {\sqrt 3 } \right)}^2}} }}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{12}}{{13}} + \frac{1}{2} = \frac{{37}}{{26}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{3} = x{/tex} and {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\sqrt 3 = y{/tex}
so that {tex}\tan x = \frac{2}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\tan y = \sqrt 3{/tex}
Therefore,
{tex}\sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{2}{3}} \right) + \cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt 3 } \right){/tex}
= sin(2x) + cos y
{tex} = \frac{{2\tan x}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}x}} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 + {{\tan }^2}y} }}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{2.\frac{2}{3}}}{{1 + \frac{4}{9}}} + \frac{1} { \sqrt{1 + {{{\left( {\sqrt 3 } \right)}^2}} }}{/tex}

solution_text: From the given equation, we have sin(sin -1 x + sin -1 (1 - x)) = sin(cos -1 x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin(sin -1 x) cos(sin -1 (1 - x)) + cos(sin -1 x)sin(sin -1 (1 - x)) = sin(cos -1 x) {tex}\Rightarrow x\sqrt {1 - {{(1 - x)}^2}} + (1 - x)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex} = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x\sqrt {2x - {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} (1 - x - 1) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x \left( {\sqrt {2x - {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0 or 2x - x 2 = 1 - x 2 {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x = 0 or {tex}x = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

question_text: Solve the equation {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}6x + {\sin ^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3x= - \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
question_html: Solve the equation {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}6x + {\sin ^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3x= - \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
solution_text: From the given equation, we have {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}6x = - \frac{\pi }{2} - {\sin ^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}6x} \right) = \sin \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2} - {{\sin }^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6x = - \cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 6x = - \sqrt {1 - 108{x^2}} {/tex} . Squaring both sides, we get 36x 2 = 1 - 108x 2 {tex}\Rightarrow 144{x^2} = 1\;\; \Rightarrow x \pm \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex} Note that {tex}x = - \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex} is the only root of the equation as {tex}x = \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex} does not satisfy it.
solution_html:
From the given equation, we have
{tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}6x = - \frac{\pi }{2} - {\sin ^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}6x} \right) = \sin \left( { - \frac{\pi }{2} - {{\sin }^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x} \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 6x = - \cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}6\sqrt 3 x} \right){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 6x = - \sqrt {1 - 108{x^2}} {/tex}.

Squaring both sides, we get
36x2 = 1 - 108x2
{tex}\Rightarrow 144{x^2} = 1\;\; \Rightarrow x \pm \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex}
Note that {tex}x = - \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex} is the only root of the equation as {tex}x = \frac{1}{{12}}{/tex} does not satisfy it.

solution_html:

= {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{2 \tan \frac{\alpha}{2} \frac{1-\tan \frac{\beta}{2}}{1+\tan \frac{\beta}{2}}}{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}\left(\frac{1-\tan \frac{\beta}{2}}{1+\tan \frac{\beta}{2}}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{2 \tan \frac{\alpha}{2}\left(1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}\right)}{\left(1+\tan \frac{\beta}{2}\right)^{2}-\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}\left(1-\tan \frac{\beta}{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{2 \tan \frac{\alpha}{2}\left(1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}\right)}{\left(1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}\right)\left(1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}\right)+2 \tan \frac{\beta}{2}\left(1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{2 \tan \frac{\alpha}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}} \frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}}}{\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\alpha}{2}}+\frac{2 \tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{\beta}{2}}}{/tex}
